crowdsourced_context.title: Remote command execution and system compromise
crowdsourced_context.details: This IP was involved in 154391 events across 27 distinct attack types. Attacks: rdp-proxy (124183), ssh-process (18871), postgresql-query (3385), tcp-portscan (2527), postgresql-login (1018), mysql-query (1014), smb3-login (828), mysql-login (648), mssql-login (601), mssql-query (282), rdp-event (183), ssh-login (178), mysql-info (131), smb-login (131), mysql-db (83), mysql-command (77), mysql-connection (69), mysql-client-detect (44), mysql-handshake-debug (42), mysql-routing (42), ftp-login (17), mysql-error (17), SIS_odtu-login (11), ssh-command (3), mysql-greeting (2), postgresql-meta (2), ssh-cisco-login (2). First seen: 2024-03-01 09:14 UTC, Last seen: 2026-02-27 10:33 UTC.

OriginAS: 
Organization: Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA)
RegDate: 
Updated: 2013-08-30
Comment: Addresses starting with "127." are used when one program needs to talk to another program running on the same machine using the Internet 
Comment: Protocol. 127.0.0.1 is the most commonly used address and is called the "loopback" address.
Comment: 
Comment: These addresses were assigned by the IETF, the organization that develops Internet protocols, in the Standard document, RFC 1122, which can 
Comment: be found here:
Comment: http://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc1122

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: Identifies executable converted using PyInstaller. This rule by itself does NOT necessarily mean the detected file is malicious.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: @bartblaze

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Rar Usage with Password and Compression Level
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the use of rar.exe, on the command line, to create an archive with password protection or with a specific compression level. This is pretty indicative of malicious actions.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Python Image Load By Non-Python Process
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the image load of "Python Core" by a non-Python process. This might be indicative of a Python script bundled with Py2Exe.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Patrick St. John, OTR (Open Threat Research)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET INFO Observed DNS Query to .life TLD
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert dns $HOME_NET any -> any any (msg:"ET INFO Observed DNS Query to .life TLD"; dns.query; content:".life"; nocase; endswith; reference:url,www.spamhaus.org/statistics/tlds/; classtype:bad-unknown; sid:2027867; rev:5; metadata:affected_product Any, attack_target Client_Endpoint, created_at 2019_08_13, deployment Perimeter, signature_severity Informational, updated_at 2022_11_21;)

crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET INFO Observed DNS Query to .life TLD
